title: Pilosella cilicica
taxonomy: Pilosella cilicica is classified within the family Rosaceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Pilosella, which is often confused with the related genus Senecio. The classification of Pilosella cilicica is further broken down into the genus Pilosella, and then into the family Rosaceae. The plant's scientific classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Rosales, Family: Rosaceae, Genus: Pilosella, Species: P. cilicica. This classification helps to identify and understand the plant's relationships with other organisms.
uses: Pilosella cilicica has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. constituents: Pilosella cilicica contains sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids as its active constituents. preparations:
Pilosella cilicica. 
Herbal preparations of Pilosella cilicica, a plant species native to Turkey, are traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and then used to make teas or infusions. These preparations are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's effectiveness and potential side effects. In Turkey, Pilosella cilicica is often used as a folk remedy for treating digestive problems and sore throats.
intro: Pilosella cilicica, also known as the Cilician hawk's-beard, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella cilicica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pilosella cilicica is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments for skin care.
